message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "U1-C1",
      "title": "Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements",
      "description": "Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty analysis, dimensional analysis, and their applications.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities",
        "Apply rules of significant figures in calculations involving measured values",
        "Determine the uncertainty in results based on instrument precision",
        "Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships between physical quantities"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Physical World and Measurement",
      "chapter_name": "Units and Measurements"
    },
    {
      "code": "U2-C1",
      "title": "Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line",
      "description": "Competencies related to describing motion using differentiation and integration, analyzing uniform and non-uniform motion, and interpreting kinematic graphs.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe motion of an object moving along a straight line using frame of reference concepts",
        "Apply calculus (differentiation/integration) to derive relations for uniformly accelerated motion",
        "Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs to analyze motion characteristics",
        "Distinguish between average speed, average velocity, and instantaneous velocity"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Straight Line"
    },
    {
      "code": "U2-C2",
      "title": "Kinematics - Motion in a Plane",
      "description": "Competencies related to vector operations, resolution of vectors, and analysis of projectile motion and uniform circular motion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors using rectangular components",
        "Calculate dot product (scalar) and cross product (vector) of two vectors in a plane",
        "Analyze the trajectory of projectiles under gravity assuming constant acceleration",
        "Derive expressions for velocity and position in uniform circular motion"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Plane"
    },
    {
      "code": "U3-C1",
      "title": "Laws of Motion - Newton's Laws and Friction",
      "description": "Competencies related to understanding inertia, momentum, impulse, conservation laws, equilibrium conditions, and frictional forces.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the concept of force and inertia using Newton's First Law",
        "Apply Newton's Second Law (F=ma) and Third Law to solve dynamics problems involving collisions",
        "Use the principle of conservation of linear momentum in isolated systems",
        "Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces including static, kinetic, rolling friction, and lubrication"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Laws of Motion",
      "chapter_name": "Laws of Motion"
    },
    {
      "code": "U4-C1",
      "title": "Work, Energy and Power - Conservation Laws",
      "description": "Competencies related to work-energy theorem, potential energy concepts, conservative forces, and collision analysis.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using integration or graphical methods",
        "Apply the Work-Energy Theorem to relate net work to change in kinetic energy",
        "Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces based on path independence of work",
        "Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions (1D and 2D) conserving momentum and/or kinetic energy"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Work, Energy and Power",
      "chapter_name": "Work, Energy and Power"
    },
    {
      "code": "U5-C1",
      "title": "System of Particles and Rotational Motion - Dynamics",
      "description": "Competencies related to center of mass motion, torque, angular momentum conservation, rotational kinematics, and moment of inertia.",
      "competencies": [
        "Determine the position of the center of mass for systems of particles and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod)",
        "Apply Newton's Second Law for rotation: Torque = I * alpha",
        "Use conservation of angular momentum to analyze rotational interactions",
        "Calculate moment of inertia and radius of gyration for simple geometrical objects"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body",
      "chapter_name": "System of Particles and Rotational Motion"
    },
    {
      "code": "U6-C1",
      "title": "Gravitation - Universal Law and Orbital Mechanics",
      "description": "Competencies related to Kepler's laws, gravitational potential energy, escape speed, and satellite motion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Newton's Universal Law of Gravitation to calculate forces between celestial bodies",
        "Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth from Earth's surface",
        "Calculate orbital velocity and escape speed for satellites orbiting a planet",
        "Relate gravitational potential energy to work done against gravitational force"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Gravitation",
      "chapter_name": "Gravitation"
    },
    {
      "code": "U7-C1",
      "title": "Properties of Bulk Matter - Solids and Fluids",
      "description": "Competencies related to elasticity, stress-strain relationships, fluid pressure, viscosity, surface tension, and Bernoulli's theorem.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Hooke's Law and calculate Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus, and Poisson's ratio",
        "Analyze the behavior of fluids under hydrostatic conditions using Pascal's law",
        "Calculate terminal velocity and apply Stokes' law for viscous drag on spherical bodies",
        "Use Bernoulli's theorem to analyze fluid flow in pipes and explain phenomena like dynamic lift"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Solids / Fluids"
    },
    {
      "code": "U7-C3",
      "title": "Thermal Properties - Heat and Thermodynamics Basics",
      "description": "Competencies related to specific heat, calorimetry, phase changes, modes of heat transfer, and blackbody radiation laws.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate heat required for temperature change using specific heat capacity (Cp, Cv)",
        "Apply the principle of calorimetry to solve problems involving mixing substances",
        "Analyze latent heat effects during phase transitions (melting/boiling)",
        "Compare conduction, convection, and radiation as modes of heat transfer"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Thermal Properties of Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "U8-C1",
      "title": "Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes",
      "description": "Competencies related to thermodynamic state variables, laws of thermodynamics, and analysis of gaseous processes.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply the Zeroth Law to define temperature and thermal equilibrium",
        "Use the First Law (conservation of energy) for closed systems involving heat and work",
        "Analyze reversible/irreversible isothermal and adiabatic processes in ideal gases",
        "Evaluate efficiency and performance of cyclic thermodynamic engines"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Thermodynamics",
      "chapter_name": "Thermodynamics"
    },
    {
      "code": "U9-C1",
      "title": "Kinetic Theory - Gas Behavior",
      "description": "Competencies related to the kinetic theory of gases, molecular speed distributions, and equipartition theorem.",
      "competencies": [
        "Derive pressure exerted by an ideal gas using kinetic theory assumptions",
        "Calculate root-mean-square (rms) speed and mean free path of gas molecules",
        "Apply the law of equipartition of energy to determine specific heat capacities of gases"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases",
      "chapter_name": "Kinetic Theory"
    },
    {
      "code": "U10-C1",
      "title": "Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion",
      "description": "Competencies related to periodic motion, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and simple pendulums.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify conditions for Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) and derive its differential equation",
        "Analyze the relationship between uniform circular motion and SHM using phase concepts",
        "Calculate time period of a loaded spring and a simple pendulum"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Oscillations"
    },
    {
      "code": "U10-C2",
      "title": "Waves - Wave Motion and Superposition",
      "description": "Competencies related to wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, beats, and interference.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and their propagation speeds"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Waves"
    },
    {
      "code": "U1-C2",
      "title": "Electrostatics - Charges, Fields, and Flux",
      "description": "Competencies related to Coulomb's law, electric fields due to various charge distributions, Gauss's theorem applications, and dipoles.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate electrostatic force between point charges using superposition principle"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–I Electrostatics",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–1: Electric Charges and Fields"
    },
    {
      "code": "U2-C3",
      "title": "Electrostatic Potential - Energy and Capacitance",
      "description": "Competencies related to electric potential, equipotential surfaces, dielectrics, capacitor combinations, and energy storage.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate electric potential due to point charges, dipoles, and systems of charges"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–I Electrostatics",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–2: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance"
    },
    {
      "code": "U3-C4",
      "title": "Current Electricity - Ohm's Law and Circuits",
      "description": "Competencies related to drift velocity, resistivity, internal resistance of cells, Kirchhoff's laws, and Wheatstone bridge.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Ohm's law to analyze linear and non-linear V-I characteristics"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-II Current Electricity",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–3: Current Electricity"
    },
    {
      "code": "U4-C5",
      "title": "Magnetism - Fields, Forces, and Torque",
      "description": "Competencies related to Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, magnetic forces on charges/conductors, torque on dipoles, and galvanometer conversion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate magnetic field due to current-carrying conductors using symmetry arguments"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–4: Moving Charges and Magnetism / Chapter–5: Magnetism and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "U5-C6",
      "title": "Electromagnetic Induction - Faraday's Laws",
      "description": "Competencies related to induced EMF, Lenz's law, self/mutual induction, AC circuits (reactance/impedance), and resonance.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction to calculate induced emf"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–6: Electromagnetic Induction / Chapter–7: Alternating Current"
    },
    {
      "code": "U6-C8",
      "title": "Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Properties",
      "description": "Competencies related to displacement current, transverse nature of EM waves, and characteristics of the electromagnetic spectrum.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the generation of electromagnetic waves through oscillating charges"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–V Electromagnetic Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–8: Electromagnetic Waves"
    },
    {
      "code": "U7-C9",
      "title": "Optics - Ray and Wave Optics",
      "description": "Competencies related to reflection/refraction, lens/mirror formulas, optical instruments, interference, diffraction, and Huygens' principle.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply mirror and lens formulas to determine image properties"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–VI Optics",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–9: Ray Optics / Chapter–10: Wave Optics"
    },
    {
      "code": "U8-C11",
      "title": "Dual Nature - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves",
      "description": "Competencies related to photoelectric effect, Einstein's equation, de-Broglie wavelength, and particle-wave duality.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the experimental observations of the photoelectric effect using quantum theory"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "U9-C12",
      "title": "Atoms - Rutherford and Bohr Models",
      "description": "Competencies related to atomic structure, scattering experiments, energy levels in hydrogen atom, and spectral lines.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe the limitations of classical physics leading to quantum models"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit-VIII Atoms and Nuclei",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–12: Atoms / Chapter–13: Nuclei"
    },
    {
      "code": "U9-C14",
      "title": "Electronic Devices - Semiconductors and Diodes",
      "description": "Competencies related to energy bands, p-n junctions, diode characteristics (forward/reverse bias), rectification, and basic semiconductor devices.",
      "competencies": [
        "Analyze the I-V characteristics of a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit–IX Electronic Devices",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits"
    }
  ]
}
